diagrams.title: Transaction State Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows the lifecycle of a database transaction through five states: Active, Partially Committed, Committed, Failed, and Aborted
elements: Arrows showing valid transitions between states
diagrams.purpose: Understanding transaction flow and state transitions for exam questions on transaction lifecycle

diagrams.title: ACID Properties Illustration
diagrams.description: Four quadrants showing each ACID property with visual icons and brief explanations
elements: Atomicity icon (light switch - all or nothing)
Examples showing each property in action
diagrams.purpose: Visual memory aid for remembering and explaining ACID properties in exams

diagrams.title: Lock Compatibility Matrix
diagrams.description: 2x2 matrix showing which lock types can coexist on the same resource
elements: Color coding: Green for compatible, Red for incompatible
diagrams.purpose: Understanding lock compatibility for concurrency control questions and deadlock scenarios

diagrams.title: Isolation Levels Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Staircase diagram showing four isolation levels from lowest (READ UNCOMMITTED) to highest (SERIALIZABLE)
elements: Problems prevented at each level marked
Trade-off arrows showing concurrency vs safety
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the trade-off between concurrency and data consistency across isolation levels
